updates_description: You can find all our latest updates here
about_journey_text:
Plebis Online started in probably the most random way possible - during the COVID-19 lockdown. Like a lot of people stuck at home, we were just looking for ways to pass the time. We ended up playing this game called Chaos (it’s on Steam, look it up!) and were having an absolute blast. At some point, one of us joked, “What if we made a game like this?”. And then... we decided to actually do it. The only catch? We were students at the time, so juggling schoolwork and game development wasn’t always easy. But that didn’t stop us. We kept going, kept learning, and slowly got better at this whole game dev thing.

January 21st was the day we had the idea. By the evening, we had the whole thing planned out - or at least, we thought we did. None of us really knew what we were doing at first. Our early models were rough, our code barely worked, and the first versions of the game were... well, let’s just say a little rough around the edges. But that didn’t stop us. We kept going, kept learning, and slowly got better at this whole game dev thing.

That being said, it wasn’t all smooth sailing. There were phases where we barely touched the game for weeks - sometimes even longer. Life got in the way, motivation dipped, and progress slowed. But no matter what, we always came back to it. After all, it was, is, and will remain a project of heart for all of us.

We ended up starting over from scratch four times (yeah, four). Each time, we took what we learned and tried to make something better. It wasn’t easy, but looking back now, seeing how far we’ve come since those early, messy days makes all the effort totally worth it.
about_team_text:
Plebis Online is the result of a group of students who decided to try their hand at game development during the COVID-19 lockdown. None of us were experts, but we were determined to learn as we went and worked together to bring our idea to life.

Ole was the main developer, responsible for most of the frontend work, he created almost all of the visual elements, the sound design and wrote most of the codebase. He took on the role of organizing and leading the team, keeping everything moving forward - even when we had no idea what we were doing.

Erik helped designing maps and thinking concepts and ideas carefully through, his main focus shifted to writing the game’s lore, website texts, and promotional materials. Although programming wasn't his strength, he ensured that the world and story of Plebis Online had depth and coherence.

Nico worked on the earlier multiplayer aspects and backend of the game. He was the one who made sure everything worked online, even if it meant dealing with bugs and issues along the way. Nico was also the one who sometimes pushed deadlines, but his work was key to making the game functional for players.

Alongside us were Hanno, who created the music and sound design, and Nils, who helped developing this website. Both of them brought their own unique skills and helped shape the experience of the game.

We’re just a group of students with a shared interest in gaming, trying our best to make something we can be proud of. It hasn’t been easy, but we’ve learned a lot along the way and are excited to share what we’ve built.
short_description: Plebis Online is your ultimate arcade shooter. Grab your friends and dive into your very own experiences: experiment with physics, rules, and gadgets, engage in fast-paced combat, and conquer a variety of interactive maps. Sit back, mess around, and enjoy the chaos together!
lore_title: Step into a chaotic, high-tech world of Roman grandeur.
general_text: Plebis Online is a fast-paced multiplayer first-person shooter where you can team up with up to 10 friends or jump into public lobbies for unique and chaotic battles. With a variety of game modes, customizable settings, and exciting gameplay, you’ll always find something fresh and fun for you and your friends.
gadgets_text: Grab a bunch of diverse Gadgets and enter the Arena: Whether you prefer stealth, active, passive, niche, broad, defence, or offence, the choice is yours. Each gadget offers unique abilities that add to your playstyle and create endless variety in the Arena.
settings_text: Explore the most match customisation you’ve ever seen: choose from a wide range of premade game modes from the base game or the workshop, or create your very own experience by setting specific rules, modifying environmental factors, or tweaking any match aspect you can imagine. Plebis Online lets you make each match unpredictable and ensures that no two rounds ever feel the same. Share your creativity and publish your creations to the workshop.
arenas_text: Dive into highly interactive and dynamic arenas: from destructible environments and environmental hazards to dangerous traps, each Arena features unique twists that keep every match exciting. The layout and design of the maps ensure interesting combat and constant adaptation to steadily changing conditions.
lore_text: Step into an alternate timeline where the Roman Empire never fell. Experience a world where ancient Rome has advanced far beyond our time. Fight in arenas that blend futuristic tech with iconic Roman architecture. The Empire's reach spans across continents, and the arenas are yours to explore and immerse yourself in.
friendship_text: Plebis Online is the product of a long-lasting friendship, made to fuel your friendships and spark new ones. Plebis Online thrives on its community; your ideas, critiques, and vision matter most to us. We are excited to welcome you to Plebis Online, a world of gaming that brings people together.
